description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:lts-alpine Docker image with three stages: tests, security, and publish. In the tests stage add a code quality job that runs 'yarn install' then 'yarn run lint', a dependencies outdated job (allow_failure) that runs 'yarn install' and 'yarn outdated', and a tests & coverage job that runs 'yarn install' and 'yarn coverage', stores the coverage/ directory as an artifact and extracts coverage percentage with the regex /All files[^|]*\|[^|]*\s+([\d\.]+)/. In the security stage add a security audit job that depends on the three test jobs and runs 'yarn audit --level high --groups dependencies'. In the publish stage add a package job that needs the security audit job and runs 'yarn install', 'yarn build', and 'yarn pack', publishing the generated .tgz files as artifacts; a pages job that runs only on the main branch, needs the tests & coverage and security audit jobs, moves the coverage/ folder to public/, and publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact with a 30‑day expiration and environment named 'coverage report' at https://vuedoc.gitlab.io/parser; and a publish job that runs on tags or pipeline triggers, needs the security audit job, writes the NPM auth token to .npmrc, runs 'yarn install', 'yarn build', and 'yarn publish', and deploys to the npm registry at https://www.npmjs.com/package/@vuedoc/parser with environment 'npm'.
